emails.body:
Good morning,

Here’s your daily ISR and networks brief for 30 September 2026.

**Top stories**
- **Tactical Cloud Pilots Expand** – The Army’s Project Convergence‑Cloud initiative announced two new pilot sites at Fort Bragg and Joint Base Lewis‑McChord. Early results show a 27 % reduction in data‑to‑decision latency for brigade‑level targeting.
- **Joint All‑Domain Command & Control (JADC2) Integration** – The Air Force released the latest API spec for the Advanced Battle Management System (ABMS) to enable cross‑service sensor fusion. Industry partners are scheduled for a joint test event in November.
- **Commercial SATCOM Leveraging** – A new DoD‑wide contract vehicle awards $1.2 B for low‑Earth‑orbit broadband services, aimed at augmenting contested‑environment communications.
- **Cyber‑Resilient ISR Platforms** – The Navy’s MQ‑9B Reaper fleet completed a red‑team assessment highlighting firmware supply‑chain risks; mitigation guidance will be published next month.

**Quick reads**
- GAO report on ISR acquisition timelines (PDF, 12 pages)
- DARPA’s “Mosaic Warfare” demo after‑action review (video, 18 min)
- Upcoming AFCEA TechNet conference agenda (link)

Let me know if you’d like deeper dives on any of these items.

Best regards,
C4ISRNET Today Editorial Team
https://c4isrnet.example/today

Good afternoon,

Your electronic‑warfare roundup for 3 October 2026 is below.

**Spectrum Policy Updates**
- **FCC 5G Mid‑Band Reallocation** – The Commission voted 3‑2 to open 3.45‑3.55 GHz for shared federal/commercial use. DoD has 180 days to submit coexistence plans.
- **International Telecommunication Union (ITU) WRC‑27 Prep** – U.S. delegation submitted position papers on maritime‑mobile satellite service protections; interagency review due 15 Oct.

**Sensor & Platform Developments**
- **AN/ALQ‑257 Integrated Viper** – First flight test of the next‑gen airborne EW suite completed at Edwards AFB; reported 40 % improvement in broadband jamming effectiveness.
- **Ground‑Based Multi‑Function EW (GMFEW) Increment 2** – Army awarded a $340 M contract to Raytheon for enhanced direction‑finding and cognitive waveform libraries.
- **Navy’s SEWIP Block 3** – Integration testing on DDG‑51 class destroyers shows reduced false‑alarm rates in dense littoral environments.

**Analyst Commentary**
- “Spectrum sharing will be the defining challenge for joint EW over the next decade,” says Dr. Maria Chen, senior fellow at the Center for Strategic Spectrum Studies.
- Emerging AI‑driven cognitive EW tools are moving from lab to field trials; expect a joint experimentation campaign in Q1 FY27.

**Events**
- **EW Europe 2026** – 12‑14 Nov, London (virtual attendance available)
- **DoD Spectrum Management Workshop** – 22 Oct, Arlington, VA (registration closes 8 Oct)

Feel free to forward any items to your team.

Regards,
C4ISRNET Today Editorial Team
https://c4isrnet.example/today
